idea.hook: Wanna visit the Kuiper Belt? Our Solar System's distant icy ring! ❄️🪐
facts: The Kuiper Belt begins at about 30 Astronomical Units (4.5 billion km) from the Sun.
At current fastest rocket speeds (~60,000 km/h), it would take you ~8.5 years to get there.
If you could drive a car (100 km/h) to the Kuiper Belt, the journey would take ~5,137 years!
idea.payoff: Even at the speed of light, it takes ~4.2 hours. Our cosmic 'backyard' is unimaginably vast!
idea.title: To The Kuiper Belt! 🚀❄️🌌

scenes.visual: A stylized view of the outer Solar System, showing icy planets and a faint ring of distant objects beyond Neptune. Stars fill the background.

scenes.text: Kuiper Belt starts ~4.5 Billion km from Sun.
scenes.visual: An animation showing the Sun, then planets orbiting, then an expanding circle representing the Kuiper Belt's edge. Text '30 AU' briefly appears near the edge.
A sleek, stylized rocket streaks rapidly across the screen from right to left, passing by distant icy dwarf planets and stars. A subtle timer graphic appears and ticks.
A tiny, cartoonish car slowly but continuously drives from left to right across a vast, dark expanse of space. Earth appears briefly in the background and shrinks away.

scenes.text: Even at light speed, it takes ~4.2 Hours. Our cosmic 'backyard' is VAST!
scenes.visual: Rapid zoom out from a single star, revealing more stars, then a galaxy, then a cluster of galaxies, emphasizing the immense scale of space. Light streaks across the screen as it zooms out.
